body.title: agent_datacenter: create github repo and scaffold full directory structure
body.result: Repo created at https://github.com/akienm/agent_datacenter. Full scaffold committed and pushed: 39 files, all spec dirs, pyproject.toml, CLAUDE.md, README stub, .gitignore. pip install -e . passes. All Phase 1 gate deps now unblocked.
body.description:
Create the standalone agent_datacenter repo (separate from TheIgors) and scaffold the full directory tree per spec.

Problem: agent_datacenter is a portable package intended for work + every future project. It must not live inside TheIgors. The repo must exist before any code is written.

Proposed shape:
- Create ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/ locally (~/dev/src/ already exists)
- git init; create github repo akienm/agent_datacenter; set remote
- Scaffold dirs per spec: agent_datacenter/{__init__, rack, device, shim}, bus/, skeleton/, devices/{postgres,igor,claude,inference,web_server,discord_bot,swadl,installer}/, config/, logging/, tests/fixtures/
- Create pyproject.toml (pip install -e . works, no deps yet)
- CLAUDE.md bootstrapped for agent_datacenter (skills-aware)
- .gitignore (*.db, .env, __pycache__, *.pyc, datacenter_logs/)
- README.md stub: what this is, what it is NOT, link to spec
- Push initial commit to github

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/ (new repo, all scaffold files)

Design rules:
- oop-first: device.py and shim.py (abstract base classes) are the design center — stub files note this
- no-sqlite: .gitignore excludes *.db from day one
- docs-in-code: README.md covers what-this-is and what-it-is-not per spec §What This Is

Scope boundary:
IN: repo creation, directory scaffold, pyproject.toml, CLAUDE.md, README stub, .gitignore, initial push
OUT: any working code — all __init__.py and device.py files are empty stubs

Test plan:
- pip install -e . succeeds with empty stubs (no import errors)
- git clone from github works on a clean machine
- All spec-required directories exist
